title: The day rain changed our plans in Edinburgh
summary: A short story about a day when weather forced you and a friend to reinvent the afternoon.
topic: The day rain changed our plans in Edinburgh
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a day when weather changed your plans. Describe the original plan, what happened, and what you did instead. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: Last May, my sister and I planned to climb…
By eleven o'clock the rain was coming down so hard that…
Instead, we ended up spending three hours in…
sampleText: Last May, my sister and I planned to climb Arthur's Seat on our first morning in Edinburgh. We had bought new shoes the week before and prepared sandwiches the night before. When we left the hotel at nine, the sky was already grey but not heavy. By eleven o'clock, the rain was coming down so hard that we could barely see the bottom of the hill. We tried to wait under a tree for ten minutes, but our jeans were already soaked through. My sister looked at me and laughed. 'New plan?' I nodded. We walked down the hill, found a small bookshop café on a side street called Victoria Street, and ordered two large pots of tea. While the rain was hitting the windows, we read books we did not buy, talked about our grandmother, and ate a slice of dark fruit cake each. Three hours passed without us noticing. When the rain finally stopped, we stepped outside into a cleaner, brighter city. We never did climb Arthur's Seat on that trip. But when my sister phones me now, she still says, 'Do you remember that café when it rained?' I always do.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for earlier preparation
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'We had bought new shoes the week before' và 'prepared sandwiches the night before' — past perfect ('had bought') vì việc mua xảy ra trước thời điểm khởi hành (past simple).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'We had bought new shoes the week before' and 'prepared sandwiches the night before' — past perfect ('had bought') because the buying happened before the departure (past simple).

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for ongoing weather
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'the rain was coming down' và 'while the rain was hitting the windows' dùng past continuous để mô tả thời tiết kéo dài trong nền của câu chuyện.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'The rain was coming down' and 'while the rain was hitting the windows' use past continuous to describe weather as the ongoing background of the story.

sampleTranslationVi: Tháng Năm năm ngoái, chị em tôi định leo lên Arthur's Seat vào buổi sáng đầu tiên ở Edinburgh. Chúng tôi đã mua giày mới tuần trước đó và chuẩn bị bánh sandwich tối hôm trước. Khi chúng tôi rời khách sạn lúc chín giờ, bầu trời đã xám nhưng chưa nặng. Đến mười một giờ, mưa đang trút xuống dữ dội đến mức chúng tôi gần như không thể thấy chân đồi. Chúng tôi cố trú dưới một gốc cây mười phút, nhưng quần bò của chúng tôi đã ướt sũng. Chị tôi nhìn tôi và cười. 'Kế hoạch mới?' Tôi gật đầu. Chúng tôi đi xuống đồi, tìm thấy một quán cà phê kèm hiệu sách nhỏ trên một con phố nhỏ tên là Victoria Street, và gọi hai ấm trà lớn. Trong khi mưa đang đập vào cửa sổ, chúng tôi đọc những cuốn sách chúng tôi không mua, nói chuyện về bà ngoại, và mỗi người ăn một miếng bánh trái cây sẫm màu. Ba tiếng đồng hồ trôi qua mà chúng tôi không để ý. Khi mưa cuối cùng dừng, chúng tôi bước ra một thành phố sạch hơn, sáng hơn. Chúng tôi không bao giờ leo Arthur's Seat trong chuyến đi đó. Nhưng khi chị tôi gọi điện cho tôi bây giờ, chị vẫn nói, 'Cậu còn nhớ quán cà phê hôm trời mưa không?' Tôi luôn nhớ.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple opening.
options: Last May, my sister and I planned to climb Arthur's Seat.
Last May, my sister and I plan to climb Arthur's Seat.
Last May, my sister and I are planning to climb Arthur's Seat.
Last May, my sister and I have planned to climb Arthur's Seat.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'planned' for a finished past intention.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past perfect sentence.
options: We had bought new shoes the week before.
We have bought new shoes the week before.
We was bought new shoes the week before.
We had buy new shoes the week before.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had bought' marks an earlier action than the main past timeline.

options: completely wet
slightly damp
very cold
very tired

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While the rain was hitting the windows, we read books we did not buy.
While the rain hit the windows, we were read books we did not buy.
While the rain is hitting the windows, we read books we did not buy.
While the rain hits the windows, we read books we did not buy.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was hitting' for ongoing weather; past simple 'read' for the inserted action.

options: to reach a final situation or place, often unplanned
to climb to the top of something
to leave a place quickly
to finish a meal
mcQuestions.explanation: 'End up + place / -ing' = arrive at an unexpected outcome.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about the new plan.
options: Instead, we ended up spending three hours in a small café.
Instead, we ended up to spend three hours in a small café.
Instead, we ended up spent three hours in a small café.
Instead, we end up spending three hours in a small café.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'End up + -ing' is the fixed pattern; past simple 'ended'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence about heavy rain.
options: The rain was coming down so hard that we could barely see the hill.
The rain came down so hardly that we can barely see the hill.
The rain is coming down so hard that we could barely see the hill.
The rain comes down so hard that we could barely see the hill.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous + 'so hard that' result clause; 'hard' is the correct adverb here (not 'hardly').

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'without us noticing'.
options: Three hours passed without us noticing.
Three hours passed without us notice.
Three hours passed without we noticing.
Three hours passed without that we notice.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Without + object pronoun + -ing' is the natural pattern.

options: We stepped outside into a cleaner, brighter city.
We stepped outside into a more clean, more bright city.
We stepped outside into a more cleaner, more brighter city.
We stepped outside into a clean, bright more city.
to decide not to do something that was planned
to make a plan louder
to share a plan with others
to climb to the top

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural closing line.
options: Do you remember that café when it rained?
Do you remember that café when it was rained?
Do you remember that café when it raining?
Do you remembered that café when it rained?
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'Do you remember' + past simple 'rained' for a finished past event.
mcQuestions.hint: Do/does + base verb; past simple of 'rain'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do the days when plans fall apart often become the days we remember most clearly years later?
